H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere:
HEALING overstimulation DESCENT protocol with n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong; AutoGluon predicts 7.248, delta of +0.10 (non‑material).
Qualitative data shows 66% report noticeable improvement, settle within 5–10 minutes, and rate rhythm as balanced/comfortable; YouTube reach is 29 views.
The strong sample and alignment with prediction support continued use; modest gains could be amplified by increasing platform visibility.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Effectively calms overstimulation for many, especially commuters and caregivers; rhythm is a strong point.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere:
HEALING overstimulation DESCENT protocol with n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong; AutoGluon predicts 6.987, delta of +0.07 (non‑material).
Qualitative feedback indicates noticeable improvement, settle around 5–10 minutes, and rhythm described as balanced.
YouTube reach is 5 views.
Consistent performance and non‑material prediction gap suggest current design is effective; focus on discovery rather than redesign.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Similar calming effect to H-OS-L1; a small audience still finds the rhythm neutral.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier

Non‑material prediction gap and moderate variance support retaining the protocol while experimenting with softer groove options for bedtime listeners.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Calms overstimulation but some find the groove slightly busy; may need softer version for sleep.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere:
HEALING DESCENT protocol with n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong; AutoGluon predicts 7.596, indicating a material upward delta of +0.38.
Qualitative data shows 63% report moderate improvement, settle within ~10 minutes, and cite balanced rhythm; YouTube reach is minimal (5 views) limiting discovery.
Qualitative responses cluster around "balanced" and "comfortable" rhythm, with most caregivers using it in therapy sessions.
Given strong sample size and consistent positive feedback, the protocol is well positioned for broader dissemination but benefits from targeted outreach to increase view count.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners find clear relief from anxiety and stress, settling quickly with a calming rhythm.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere:

Qualitative feedback notes noticeable improvement for many, settle times of 5–20 minutes, and neutral to positive music opinions.
YouTube reach is low (6 views) suggesting limited audience exposure.
The protocol exhibits a narrower distribution than L1 but still underperforms AutoGluon expectations, indicating potential for design fine‑tuning of rhythm intensity.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Offers moderate stress relief; some find the music neutral but still report improvement.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The data reveals broadly positive listener outcomes across the three series, with HEALING showing the most consistent efficacy and TRANSFORMING exhibiting expected higher variance.
• HEALING’s DESCENT protocols achieve strong scores (mean 7.22, n=67) and high confidence, supported by qualitative reports of balanced rhythm and quick settlement.
• THRIVING’s HOLD protocols vary widely, with Summit (mean 7.63) standing out but limited to 35 respondents.
• TRANSFORMING’s ASCENT protocols show the greatest spread, including outliers such as Cosmos (mean 5.7) and high standard deviations.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Protocol type patterns align with design intent across series.
• HEALING DESCENT dominates with the highest mean scores and lowest variance.
• THRIVING HOLD shows widest mean range (6.46–7.63) and occasional ceiling effects.
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT consistently trails with lower means and higher dispersion, especially in Motivation and Mind clarity arcs.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Notable deviations from expected patterns.
• X-MC-L3: mean 5.7, std 2.45, bimodal low tail suggests subgroup dissatisfaction.
• T-FC-L3: mean 7.63 exceeds typical HOLD expectations but only 35 responses (n<50) raises uncertainty.
• X-MD-L3: mean 7.15 with std 2.38 indicates extreme variability within an ASCENT protocol.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Opportunities to refine protocols based on signal patterns.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10): bimodal distribution warrants an L4 variant with gentler hz slope and softer fade‑out.
• H-CR-L3 (n=27): high variance (std 1.91) suggests investigating subgroup effects via targeted survey items.
• T-MB-L2 (n=22): high std (2.03) indicates heterogeneous outcomes; adding expectation questions could clarify responder profiles.
• X-ME-L3 (n=18): AutoGluon predicts 5.7 vs observed 6.28; consider an L2 rhythm‑complexity tweak to boost early engagement.
• X-MD-L3 (n=13): mean 7.15 but std 2.38; test alternative ASCENT arcs to reduce dispersion.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analysis signals instability across most protocols.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 29 of 30 protocols show cosine similarity <0.85 compared to earlier runs, indicating analytical drift.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: HEALING DESCENT protocols cluster tightly (sim 0.91), while CR crisis protocols isolate with lowest mean similarity (0.66).
• Confidence note: Embedding data currently covers only 29 protocols and spans a short 6‑day window; interpret with caution.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Most listeners find clear relief from anxiety and stress, settling quickly with a calming rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Offers moderate stress relief; some find the music neutral but still report improvement.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Provides anxiety relief with a slightly slower settle; rhythm described as balanced.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Helps pull people out of crisis within minutes; many rate the rhythm as balanced.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Offers moderate relief during acute distress; rhythm is generally well received.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Crisis protocol shows mixed settlement; some feel immediate benefit while others need more time.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Effectively calms overstimulation for many, especially commuters and caregivers; rhythm is a strong point.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Similar calming effect to H-OS-L1; a small audience still finds the rhythm neutral.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Calms overstimulation but some find the groove slightly busy; may need softer version for sleep.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Helps many settle for sleep quickly; most report noticeable improvement and balanced rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Good sleep starter; a few find the rhythm slightly busy but overall settle within 20 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Reliable sleep unwind; rhythm is mostly neutral with some mixed feedback.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Creative flow starter with gentle lift; settles quickly and music is generally liked.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Flow stays steady with noticeable improvement for many; music reception is positive.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Peak creative uplift for some; music is loved and focus reports are high.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus protocol under‑delivers on clarity; some find rhythm a bit busy for pure focus.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Reaches a sweet spot for focus gains; minor busy‑rhythm concerns persist.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Top focus performer; users love the rhythm and report strong gains.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Maintains balance for many, though some need longer to settle; rhythm stays neutral.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Balance feels varied; some find rhythm neutral, others slightly busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Small group experiences difficulty settling; a lighter variant could help.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Mind clarity opener works for most but a few feel it worsened their state; rhythm is balanced.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Gentle descent brings noticeable clarity gains; rhythm is generally well received.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Clarity protocol underperforms; some find rhythm too busy and report negative effects.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation starter shows mixed results; some see improvement while others feel stuck.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Motivation arc trails its sibling; groove may be too slow for study contexts.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Switchback shows a bounce‑back with moderate improvement; rhythm is balanced.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Broad mood‑energy lift; caregivers use it during daily tasks and report positive rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Sunrise provides steady energy gains; some settle later, but overall rhythm is liked.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Energy arc starts low with notable no‑effect cases; rhythm may need tighter tuning.
